core: The 20-ppg 2024 season is the truest signal in the data; 2025's dip to 15.8 came with the elite weeks intact — a third of his starts still broke slates. Bell-cow usage in an offense built around him.
Back-to-back 19+ ppg seasons and an 85% hit rate — the rare back who's both explosive and dependable. Detroit's committee talk never actually capped him.
Three straight seasons of ~23 ppg — the rushing floor makes his bad days everyone else's good days. Age 30 is the only argument anyone has left against him.
The workload finally matched the talent: 19.7 ppg with elite games in 35% of starts, climbing three straight years. A new coordinator is the lone variable in an otherwise settled bell-cow picture.
Quietly climbed to 19.5 ppg while missing in only 15% of starts — target-hog usage with almost no empty weeks. The production keeps rising each year he's in this offense.
The breakout is real and steep — 7.0 to 11.9 to 17.8 ppg with a 94 opportunity score, the heaviest target load in the sport. New coordinator inherits a passing game that runs through him.
The floor merchant's floor merchant — three straight years in the 15-17 ppg band on relentless volume. What he lacks in spike weeks he repays in never losing you one.
Jumped from 14.2 to 20.8 ppg in year two, though only 7% of starts hit elite — the production is volume-steady, not ceiling-driven yet. The arrow points one direction.
The 9.4-ppg 2025 is the worst season of his career and screams situation, not skill — the 92 opportunity score says the targets never left. At 27, the résumé argues the cliff is a mirage.
A 91% hit rate and 18.6 ppg — tight end production that behaves like a WR1's. Three straight years of growth; the new coordinator is inheriting the league's most reliable weapon at the position.
Missed five games and still profiled like a top-tier option — 28% elite weeks at 23. The 2025 injury interrupts the story; it doesn't change it.
The rookie year was volume without polish — 12.9 ppg but busts in nearly half his starts. Everything rides on whether year two smooths the weekly variance, because the workload is already his.

Only 8 games in 2025, and the durability file is now thick — but the 22.5-ppg 2024 shows what a full season still looks like. A per-game elite with an availability discount baked into every conversation.
Sliding from 19.9 to 14.2 to 12.6 ppg across three seasons — the trend is the concern, not the talent. The Dallas passing volume that made him a monster hasn't been there since 2023.
Climbed to 18.7 ppg in year two, though 44% of starts still missed — the weekly variance is the last unpolished edge. The rushing contribution keeps the floor from ever fully collapsing.

The 16.8-ppg 2025 in an injury-shortened 13 games sits far below the 25.8 masterpiece before it. A new coordinator arrives with the question every 28-year-old rushing QB eventually faces: how much of the legs survive.
Rebounded to 20.1 ppg — his best per-game mark in three years — even as 37% of starts missed. The arm talent hasn't gone anywhere; the weekly consistency of the dynasty years has.
Three seasons between 15 and 18 ppg on a snap diet that never became a true bell-cow load. A new coordinator in Miami decides whether the efficiency finally meets full volume.
Seven games in 2025 cut the sample short, but a quarter of his career starts have hit elite — dual-threat production that travels. The injury year is noise until proven otherwise.

Climbed back to 20 ppg with elite games in a third of his starts — the 89 opportunity score says Indianapolis has re-committed completely. At 27, he's producing like the age curve hasn't read his file yet.
Nine rookie games at 13.3 ppg on a first-rounder's workload before the season was cut short. Small sample, heavy usage, new coordinator — the profile is all runway and unknowns.
When he plays, the targets are massive — an 87 opportunity score — but 45% of starts missed and he's lost chunks of two seasons. The talent-to-output gap is the entire evaluation.
Three seasons pinned between 16.7 and 18.1 ppg — high-floor, low-spike production that a new coordinator will try to unlock. The arm has always promised more weeks than the offense delivers.
Three straight years of growth to 17.2 ppg with a rising share of Buffalo's backfield. Approaching 27 with tread building — the production window and the age window are now the same conversation.
The 20.1-ppg 2025 is the season the draft pedigree always promised, though only 7% of starts hit elite — it was accumulation, not eruption. Consistency arrived before the ceiling did.
The move to Dallas unlocked a career-best 14.3 ppg on an 86 opportunity score, but 42% of starts still missed. Boom-bust wiring on a genuine alpha target share.
An 88 opportunity score as a rookie — Carolina force-fed him immediately — but 10.5 ppg and a 47% miss rate show the efficiency hasn't caught the usage. The role is secured; the polish isn't.
Four games in 2025 before the injury ended it, after a 14.5-ppg rookie season built on pure target gravity. At 22, the résumé is short, loud, and interrupted — with a new coordinator waiting.
A rookie tight end asked to matter immediately — 10.3 ppg with 19% elite weeks but misses in over half his starts. The flashes at 22 are the argument; the variance is the cost.
Nine games in 2025 at a career-best 19.9 ppg — efficient as ever when upright. Three seasons of near-identical per-game output make him the most stable QB profile nobody calls elite.
Back-to-back seasons near 18 ppg to start a career — steady, unspectacular, and durable. The 35% miss rate is the gap between good starter and weekly difference-maker.
The 19.2-ppg 2025 is his lowest in three years, and now a new coordinator inherits the league's most rushing-dependent QB profile at 28. The floor is still elite; the direction is the question.
The #3 pick lands in a backfield Arizona just cleared for him — no NFL snaps yet, but the draft capital says the plan is a full workload from day one.
A rookie tight end who got real usage — 11.1 ppg across a full 17 games. The position's learning curve says year-one volume like this usually precedes the year-two leap.
Slipped to 12.7 ppg after consecutive ~14.7 seasons, with a third of starts missing — the elite-week rate never matched the talent narrative. Still commands a top-tier target share at 27.
Fourteen rookie starts at 17.4 ppg — genuinely productive from the jump, with a new coordinator arriving to build around it. Short résumé, but nothing in it reads backup.
Rebounded to a career-best 13.6 ppg on an 88 opportunity score after the concussion-wrecked 2024. The route-running was never the question — availability and the offense around him were.
Eight games, 15.2 ppg, and elite weeks in a third of his career starts — per-snap production that rivals anyone's. The file is all interruptions: what exists of it is outstanding.
Two seasons stuck in the 15s after the 18.9 breakout, with only 7% of starts hitting elite — the 2023 ceiling hasn't reappeared. The talent-flash-to-steady-output conversion is still incomplete.
Two straight seasons around 14.5 ppg as Cincinnati's clear lead back — unspectacular weekly output on very spectacular volume. The role, not the ceiling, is what he offers.
Was breaking out — 14.7 ppg through 8 games — before the injury stopped it. Three straight years of growth at tight end, where late-blooming arcs like this are the position's signature.
Bounced back to 18.3 ppg over a full 17 at age 32 — still a weekly starter, now with the shortest runway among top QB assets. Every year from here is a lease, not a purchase.
The Kansas City move follows his worst season — 10.4 ppg with a 43% miss rate and a shrinking role. The vision and burst that made him are intact; the situation that suppressed him just changed.
The rookie year split the difference — 18% elite weeks against misses in half his starts, classic explosive-back-in-a-committee output. The flashes are real; the workload isn't yet.
Three straight years of decline from 20.1 to 14.7 ppg, but an 82% hit rate — the volume is eroding while the reliability isn't. A floor back whose ceiling left first.
The rookie year barely happened — 7 ppg and misses in 80% of starts buried in a crowded room. Second-round pedigree at 22 buys patience the production hasn't earned yet.
New England buys the target monopoly he never quite had in Philadelphia — though the 12.1-ppg 2025 was his quietest year since his rookie season. At 29, the physicality profile ages better than most.

The 21.5-ppg age-30 season on a 99 opportunity score is one of the great late-career workloads ever recorded — sandwiched around a lost 2024. Nobody's ceiling is higher; nobody's actuarial table is worse.
Two straight seasons nearer 10 ppg than 15, with misses in nearly two-thirds of starts — Denver is betting the Miami role, not the player, was the problem. The speed never left the profile.

Ticked up to 12.1 ppg but missed in exactly half his starts — heavy usage in a run-first ecosystem that caps receiver spikes. A new coordinator is the most interesting thing to happen to his target quality.
